summary: Matched several small MSL runtime pieces by replacing assembly with C for abort/exit handling, console UART I/O, and trig wrapper functions. The PR moved text objects from asm to src where matched, while leaving remaining unmatched assembly/data such as abort_exit dtors and trigf core math assembly in place.
what_changed: Added src/MSL/abort_exit.c implementing exit and moved the matched exit logic out of asm/MSL/abort_exit.s.
Kept the .dtors data block in asm/MSL/abort_exit.s while removing the matched text, bss, and sbss definitions from that assembly file.
Added src/MSL/console_io.c implementing func_80325F18, __write_console, and __read_console, replacing the deleted asm/MSL/console_io.s file.
Added src/MSL/trigf.c implementing tanf plus cos__Ff and sin__Ff wrappers, while leaving the remaining cosf/sinf math assembly in asm/MSL/trigf.s.
Deleted asm/MSL/string.s, which only contained two sdata constants lbl_804D5B58 and lbl_804D5B5C; the diff excerpt does not show where those constants moved.
Updated obj_files.mk to build src/MSL/abort_exit.c.o, src/MSL/console_io.c.o, and src/MSL/trigf.c.o, replacing console_io.s.o and pairing C objects with remaining split assembly where needed.
smart_moves: Split partially matched files carefully: abort_exit.s retained the .dtors section while exit moved to C, and trigf.s retained the unmatched cosf/sinf bodies while simple wrapper functions moved to C.

Preserved object ordering in obj_files.mk by inserting the new MSL C objects adjacent to the corresponding old assembly objects, important for decomp projects where link order affects addresses and data layout.
Used small global variables and arrays with existing lbl_ names to preserve layout and symbol references before better names or headers were available.
Matched tanf using the same wrapper-call structure visible in assembly: tanf calls sin__Ff and cos__Ff instead of directly calling sinf/cosf.
In console I/O, introduced local unused arrays and explicit status temporaries, likely to reproduce stack frame/register behavior from the original assembly.
decomp_lessons: When matching MSL/library code, it is acceptable to migrate only the matched text from an assembly file and leave special sections such as .dtors in assembly until the data layout is better understood.
For small runtime wrappers, preserve intermediate wrapper symbols rather than simplifying the C, because direct simplification can change call targets and generated code.
Build-file changes are part of the match: replacing asm objects with src objects must maintain the original order around neighboring Runtime/MSL objects.
Unknown global labels can remain as lbl_ addresses in early matches, especially for sbss/bss function-pointer tables and runtime state, as long as sizes and ordering are preserved.
For console/UART stubs, simple C can match assembly when initialization status, cached initialized flag, output byte count mutation, and CR-terminated read loop are modeled exactly.
naming_conventions: Existing unknown globals retained lbl_ names such as lbl_804D7060, lbl_804D7064, lbl_804D7068, lbl_804D706C, lbl_804D7070, lbl_804A2F48, lbl_804A3048, and lbl_804D7080.
MSL/libc-style exported names kept their original names: exit, tanf, cosf, sinf, __write_console, __read_console.
CodeWarrior-style C++ mangled helper names were preserved literally as cos__Ff and sin__Ff.
Temporary local variables in the new C use behavior-oriented names such as uart_status, init_status, read_status, return_status, and bytes_to_read.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used function-pointer arrays of size 64 for lbl_804A2F48 and lbl_804A3048 to replace 0x100-byte bss skips from assembly.
Modeled pre-decrement destructor stack behavior with lbl_804A2F48[--lbl_804D7064]() and lbl_804A3048[--lbl_804D7068]().
Represented the dtors walk as a pointer loop over extern void (*lbl_803B7260[])(void), stopping at NULL.
Kept nullable exit hooks as function pointers, called them when non-NULL, then cleared them to NULL to match the assembly stores.
Used InitializeUART(0xE100) and cached BOOL lbl_804D7080 to mirror the console initialization guard.
Used explicit return values 0/1 for console read/write status instead of relying on bool simplification.
Used wrapper functions cos__Ff and sin__Ff that call cosf and sinf so tanf compiles to a sin-over-cos quotient through those wrappers.
follow_up_queries: Where did the string.s sdata constants lbl_804D5B58 and lbl_804D5B5C move after this PR?
Were lbl_804A2F48 and lbl_804A3048 later named as atexit/dtor stacks?
Did later PRs replace the remaining asm/MSL/trigf.s cosf/sinf implementations with C?
Were console_io prototypes later moved into a shared MSL or dolphin header?
Did later cleanup rename lbl_804D7080 to a UART initialized flag?
